_comment: Power BI estate for the applied series. Metadata only — no patient rows. The DOMAIN, fields, KPIs, DAX, branding, and validation rules are the real ones from spectrumefficiencylimited/power-bi-template (config/examples/hospital_er.yaml). This models the *before* state: an evolved ER reporting estate whose entropy is, concretely, the things that repo's validation_rules guard against (patient count not DISTINCTCOUNT, DateTime used in joins, no calendar table, hard-coded SLA, Patient Details without RLS). Northvale is a stand-in hospital; nothing here is a real organisation.
estate: Northvale Hospital — Emergency Department reporting (synthetic, real ER pattern)

nodes.name: % Seen < 30 min
nodes.note: 30 and the 85% target are hard-coded in three visuals

nodes.name: Triage_Capacity.xlsx
nodes.refreshedBy: a charge nurse, by hand, each shift

nodes.name: satisfaction_survey.csv
nodes.refreshedBy: exported from the survey tool manually

nodes.name: DateTime used as join key
nodes.note: Patient Admission DateTime joins fact to a derived table; there is no calendar table, so time intelligence is unreliable.

edges.note: queries PAS operational tables directly; no governed model between

observations: 'Number of Patients' is defined three ways across three models — DISTINCTCOUNT (correct, ER Reporting Model), COUNT, and COUNTROWS. The repo's own rule is explicit: patient count must be DISTINCTCOUNT to avoid duplicate counting. The Executive Monthly report is wired to the COUNT version.

Exec Summary joins on Patient Admission DateTime and has no calendar table — violating no_datetime_joins and require_calendar. Time intelligence (month-over-month, same-period-last-year) is therefore unreliable.
The 30-minute SLA threshold and the 85% target are hard-coded inside three visuals instead of being parameterised.
Patient Details exposes row-level patient data with no RLS configured — a HIPAA-critical gap.
Triage_Capacity.xlsx on SharePoint, updated by a charge nurse each shift, is load-bearing for the Capacity report.
Exec Summary is the largest model (1.28 GB, 26 tables), refreshes hourly in 47 min, and is uncertified.
